title: Review & setlist: Diana Krall brought a special kind of magic to The Cabot
author: Marc Hirsh
bullets: Diana Krall performed at The Cabot in Beverly on March 28, 2026, accompanied by bassist John Clayton.
The concert featured Krall's renditions of classics like 'Almost Like Being In Love' and 'I've Got You Under My Skin.'
Krall closed the 85-minute performance with 'Come Dance With Me,' showcasing her piano skills and vocal talents.

title: Chelsea teen charged with murder after allegedly chasing down, stabbing man
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Sergio Josue Castellanos Cerrato, 18, charged with murder after alleged involvement in fatal stabbing of Eduardo Alfredo Rosales Lopez.
Incident occurred near Chestnut Street and Everett Avenue in Chelsea, resulting in multiple stabbing victims, police reported.
Castellanos Cerrato held without bail, with court appearance scheduled for April 21; police seek further information from public.

title: 2025 marked Boston area’s slowest year of post-pandemic population growth, data shows
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston area's population growth dropped to 11,991 in 2025, the slowest since 2021, Census data shows.
Massachusetts saw state population growth decrease to 15,524 in 2025, from 65,081 the previous year.
Counties like Middlesex and Worcester had reduced growth in 2025; immigration decline noted as a factor.

title: North Atlantic right whales now have a GoFundMe
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: The Marineland Right Whale Project started a GoFundMe to help protect North Atlantic right whales, raising $630 of the $15,000 goal.
Funds will support research, aerial and vessel surveys, and purchase equipment to mitigate whale deaths from vessel strikes and fishing gear entanglements.
North Atlantic right whales are critically endangered, with only a few hundred left, mostly threatened by vessel strikes and fishing gear entanglements.

title: 5 times the Patriots traded for wide receivers in recent history, and how each acquisition panned out
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Randy Moss set an NFL record for 23 touchdowns in a single season after his 2007 acquisition.
Wes Welker became a prolific slot receiver for the Patriots, complementing Randy Moss well.
Josh Gordon showed potential but off-field issues limited his impact with the Patriots.

title: Alex Cora not harping on late-game ejection by C.B. Bucknor: ‘We’re all human’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox manager Alex Cora was ejected in the eighth inning against the Reds for disputing a strike call.
Cora expressed frustration that umpire C.B. Bucknor didn't consult the first base umpire on Trevor Story's check swing.
Roman Anthony lost Boston's final ABS challenge early, affecting the team's ability to dispute calls in the close game.

title: Trevor Story reacts to strike three call that got Alex Cora ejected in Red Sox’ loss
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Trevor Story criticized umpire CB Bucknor for a controversial strike three call in the Red Sox's 6-5 loss.
Alex Cora was ejected after arguing with Bucknor, who didn't consult the first base umpire on Story's check swing.
Despite Story's strikeout, the Red Sox tied the game but ultimately lost in the 11th inning to fall to a 1-1 record.

title: Brimming with motivation after missing Olympics, Pavel Zacha is playing his best hockey for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Pavel Zacha has scored 11 goals and 19 points in 16 games since the NHL Olympic break.
An injury prevented Zacha from joining Czechia at the Olympics, serving as motivation for his recent strong performance.
Zacha's career-high 26 goals have significantly contributed to the Bruins' success and potential playoff berth.

title: Here’s what Red Sox SP Sonny Gray said about his rough debut
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Sonny Gray struggled in his Red Sox debut, conceding four runs over four innings against the Cincinnati Reds.
Manager Alex Cora acknowledged Gray's rough start but praised his ability to settle in after the first inning.
The Red Sox bullpen performed well, keeping the team competitive until a walk-off hit in the 11th inning.

title: Tens of thousands rally at Boston Common for third ‘No Kings’ protest
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Over 150,000 people attended the third 'No Kings' rally on Boston Common as part of a national demonstration.
The event featured speeches from Gov. Maura Healey, Sen. Elizabeth Warren, and performances by the Dropkick Murphys.
Organizers set up 'action tables' for attendees to engage with groups like the ACLU of Massachusetts and Indivisible Mass Coalition.
